title: Riot Platforms Reports Full Year 2023 Financial Results, Current Operational and Financial Highlights
text: Riot achieved record results in 2023, generating all-time highs of $281 million in total revenues, 6,626 Bitcoin produced, and $71 million in power credits earned from their unique power strategy. For fiscal year 2023, Riot reported total revenue of $280.7 million, as compared to $259.2 million for the same period in 2022, primarily driven by higher Bitcoin production and higher price for Bitcoin. The company produced 6,626 Bitcoin in 2023, as compared to 5,554 during the same twelve-month period in 2022, a 19% increase. Bitcoin Mining revenue was $189.0 million, as compared to $156.9 million during the same twelve-month period in 2022, with the increase driven by slightly higher values of Bitcoin mined in 2023, which averaged $28,859 per Bitcoin as compared to an average price of $28,245 per Bitcoin in 2022. Riot achieved significant progress across key strategic development targets, including completion of their 700 megawatt Rockdale Facility expansion, successful scaling of their power strategy which drove their industry-leading low cost to mine in FY 2023 to $7,539 per Bitcoin, a landmark partnership with MicroBT to lock-in a long-term, fixed-price supply of latest-generation miners, and ongoing development of their 1 gigawatt Corsicana Facility.
